hypothesis: If universal equivariant graph-based machine-learning interatomic potentials trained on broad materials datasets are applied with dispersion corrections to metal–organic frameworks and related nanoporous crystals, then they will reproduce DFT (PBE-D3) reference geometries, maintain 300 K NpT stability over 50 ps, and predict bulk moduli and 300 K heat capacities with DFT-level accuracy because their learned representations capture transferable interatomic physics across chemistries.
